A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship offers significant financial aid but requires focused work by Thursday, while the fun option is available now with no obligations.
Starting the scholarship application now builds a buffer against potential delays, reducing future stress without sacrificing any available fun time later.
I made slow progress on the scholarship application, but half my free block is already gone with most of the work still undone.
I made slow progress on the scholarship application, leaving 1.5 hours of work with only three days remaining. The missed casual game session means I cannot recover that lost leisure time.
After 1.5 hours of work, I am halfway through the scholarship application with three days left, though my low energy and sleep debt remain a concern.
After 1.5 hours of work, I've reached 63% progress on the scholarship application with three days remaining, though my sleep debt and stress levels remain a concern.
I've reached 69% progress on the scholarship application after another 1.5 hours, but my low energy and sleep debt remain concerning with only three days left.
I have reached 75% progress on the scholarship application after working for 1.5 hours, though my energy is depleted and sleep debt persists.
I slept 8 hours and now have full energy with no sleep debt, resolving the concerns from my recent work sessions on the scholarship application.
The scholarship application is now fully complete with just three days remaining. My energy is high and sleep debt is zero, leaving me ready for the next task.
I let the moment pass at 10:30 without acting, despite having a completed scholarship application and high energy.
The scholarship application is effectively finished, resolving the primary deadline pressure. With high energy and no sleep debt, I am now free to pursue other priorities.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
